title: asplenium cuneifolium subsp. dacicum
intro: Asplenium cuneifolium subsp. dacicum is a subspecies of the Asplenium cuneifolium species, commonly known as the wedge-shaped spleenwort, a type of fern native to Eastern Europe and parts of Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Asplenium cuneifolium subsp. dacicum include relief from congestion, sore throats, and skin irritations, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Asplenium cuneifolium subsp. dacicum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes dried and used in capsule or tablet form.
